message:
test(wills-estate): make viz smoke checks prove charts loaded

Cross-review area: recent wills article visualization/test changes after the loading skeleton was added around the dynamic viz wrapper.

Tighten the generic smoke assertions so they wait for each visualization's actual loaded copy instead of accepting a visible wrapper with no fallback text.

Build passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1db.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1db","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T02:27:12.627362067Z","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1lj","title":"Design and build TldrHero component","description":"# Task: Design and Build TldrHero Component ## Overview Create the hero section for the /tldr page - the first thing users see. Must immediately communicate what this page is about and draw them in. ## Content ### Headline "The Agentic Coding Flywheel" or similar - Compelling, clear, memorable - Matches site's confident tone ### Subheadline Brief explanation of the flywheel concept: "Seven tools that work together to supercharge multi-agent coding workflows. Each tool makes the others more powerful." ### Visual Element Either: - Animated flywheel graphic (spinning gear/wheel with tool icons) - Mini version of synergy diagram - Abstract particles/motion suggesting interconnection - Tool icons orbiting a center point ### Call-to-Action - "Explore the tools" → smooth scroll to tool grid - "View synergies" → scroll to synergy diagram ## Visual Design - Full-width, generous height (50-70vh) - Background gradient or subtle pattern - Large, bold typography - Centered layout - Tool icons floating/orbiting as decoration ## Animation - Headline entrance: fade up - Subheadline: fade up with delay - Visual element: continuous subtle animation - CTA buttons: appear last - Parallax scroll effect (subtle) ## Component Structure ```tsx // Gradient + decorations ``` ## Props Interface ```typescript interface TldrHeroProps { title?: string; subtitle?: string; onScrollToTools?: () => void; className?: string; } ``` ## Implementation Notes - Follow existing Hero component pattern - Use Framer Motion for animations - useInView for triggering animations - Smooth scroll behavior ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Visually striking first impression - [ ] Communicates page purpose immediately - [ ] Animations are polished - [ ] CTA scrolls smoothly to content - [ ] Responsive design - [ ] Reduced motion support","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:15:56.427136450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T19:46:05.018158162Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T19:46:05.018158162Z","close_reason":"Completed as part of TLDR page implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} -{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1uxr","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz click still fails to pin hover-previewed card","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:12.938075748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:21.070456559Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1uxr","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5h5z","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:12.938075748Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1uxr","title":"AntiPatternCardsViz click still fails to pin hover-previewed card","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:12.938075748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:25.013844362Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:24.966393781Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1uxr","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5h5z","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:12.938075748Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-21e","title":"Add repo_updater to content.ts (28 stars)","description":"## What Add repo_updater project to the projects array in jeffreyemanuel-site/lib/content.ts ## Project Details - **Name**: repo_updater (or "Repo Updater") - **GitHub**: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/repo_updater - **Stars**: 28 - **Description**: "A beautiful, automation-friendly CLI for synchronizing GitHub repositories. Keep dozens of repos in sync with a single command." - **User specifically mentioned this repo** ## Background & Context - CLI tool for managing multiple GitHub repositories - Designed for users with many repos (like Jeffrey with 70+ repos) - "Automation-friendly" suggests JSON output and scriptability - Part of developer workflow tooling - Research at /data/projects/repo_updater/ ## Implementation ```typescript { name: "Repo Updater", kind: "oss", badge: "28 stars", href: "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/repo_updater", short: "Beautiful CLI for keeping dozens of GitHub repos in sync.", description: "A polished, automation-friendly tool for synchronizing multiple GitHub repositories. Ideal for developers managing large collections of repos who need batch updates, status checks, and coordination across projects.", tags: ["CLI", "GitHub", "DevTools", "Automation", "Rust"], // verify language } ``` ## Considerations - Emphasize the "beautiful" / polished nature per description - Highlight automation-friendly aspects - May be written in Rust (common for Jeffrey's CLIs) - verify ## Acceptance Criteria - Project appears in projects array - Description highlights both UX polish and automation features - Tags accurately reflect technology","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:25:08.925931093Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:14:20.534507743Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:14:20.534507743Z","close_reason":"Added Repo Updater to content.ts with 28 stars badge","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-24j","title":"Research NTM (Named Tmux Manager) source code","description":"# Task: Research NTM (Named Tmux Manager) Source Code ## Purpose Review the NTM source code to extract accurate information for the /tldr page. ## Source Location - Path: /data/projects/ntm - Language: Go - GitHub: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm ## Research Questions ### Functionality 1. What exactly does NTM do? (Read main.go, cmd/*) 2. How does it interact with tmux? (What tmux commands?) 3. What agent types does it support? (Claude, Codex, Gemini?) 4. How does broadcasting work? 5. What capture/output features exist? ### Implementation 1. What Go libraries are used? (Bubble Tea confirmed?) 2. How is the TUI implemented? 3. What's the architecture? (Commands, services, etc.) 4. Performance characteristics? 5. Binary size, dependencies? ### Integrations 1. Does it import/integrate with Agent Mail? 2. Does it integrate with SLB? 3. Does it work with CASS? 4. How are hooks configured? ## Deliverable Structured summary in this format: ```markdown ## NTM (Named Tmux Manager) ### What It Does [2-3 sentences based on actual code] ### Why It's Useful [2-3 sentences - value prop] ### Implementation Highlights - Language: Go - Key tech: [specific libraries] - Performance: [specific metrics if available] - Binary: [single file? size?] ### Synergies - Agent Mail: [specific integration found in code] - SLB: [specific integration] - CASS: [specific integration] ``` ## Execution Use subagent with Explore capability to review source code systematically. Focus on: README.md, main.go, cmd/*, any integration files.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:16:40.418690786Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:47.385353761Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:38:47.385288510Z","close_reason":"Content for this tool already exists in lib/content.ts with comprehensive whatItDoes, whyItsUseful, implementationHighlights, synergies, techStack, keyFeatures, and useCases.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-27n","title":"Build GitHub activity API integration","description":"## Overview Extend the existing lib/github-stats.ts to include activity data for the heartbeat widget. ## IMPORTANT: File Location **Extend lib/github-stats.ts** - Per AGENTS.md, do not create new files when existing files can be extended. The github-stats.ts file already has the infrastructure we need. ## Current lib/github-stats.ts Already Has - fetchGitHubStats() returning totalStars, repoCount, followers, fetchedAt - GitHub API integration with token support - Caching with revalidate - Error handling with fallback values - formatNumber() utility ## Data to ADD to Existing Interface ```typescript // Extend GitHubStats interface in lib/github-stats.ts export interface GitHubStats { totalStars: number; repoCount: number; followers: number; fetchedAt: number; // NEW fields: latestCommitAt?: string; // ISO timestamp of most recent commit starsThisWeek?: number; // Stars gained in last 7 days (if feasible) isActive: boolean; // true if commit in last 7 days } ``` ## Implementation Approach 1. Modify existing fetchGitHubStats() function 2. Add a secondary API call to get recent events/commits 3. Use GitHub Events API: GET /users/{username}/events 4. Parse for PushEvent to find latest commit ## Caching Strategy (already in place) - Current: revalidate: 3600 (1 hour) - keep this - Fallback to stale data on API failure - already implemented - Extend fallback values for new fields ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] GitHubStats interface extended with new fields - [ ] fetchGitHubStats() returns activity data - [ ] No new files created - all changes in lib/github-stats.ts - [ ] Fallback values provided for new fields - [ ] Rate limiting respected","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:44.877297254Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.711827138Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.711827138Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit b1cb57c: Two Worlds section with parallax effects, skill transfer cards; GitHub Heartbeat widget with live activity feed, animated heartbeat visualization, streak badges","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} @@ -81,7 +81,7 @@ {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5io","title":"Feature: Mobile bottom sheet for tool details","description":"# Mobile Bottom Sheet for Tool Details ## Overview Replace inline card expansion with a bottom sheet modal on mobile devices. This provides a better mobile UX by not pushing content around and giving more room for details. ## Problem with Current Approach - Expanding a card pushes all content below it down - User loses context of where they were in the grid - Expanded content is cramped in card width - Difficult to compare tools (can only expand one at a time) ## Proposed Solution On mobile (< 768px), clicking "Show More" opens a bottom sheet that: - Slides up from bottom - Contains full tool details - Has close button and swipe-to-dismiss - Leaves card grid visible but dimmed behind ## Design Specifications ### Bottom Sheet Anatomy ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ (Dimmed backdrop) │ │ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ ← Drag handle │ ═══════ │ │ [Icon] Tool Name [✕ Close] │ │ Full tool name • ★ 1.4K │ ├─────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ What It Does │ │ [Full description text] │ │ │ │ Why It's Useful │ │ [Full explanation] │ │ │ │ Key Features │ │ • Feature 1 │ │ • Feature 2 │ │ │ │ [View on GitHub →] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Interaction States - **Opening**: Slides up with spring physics - **Open**: ~80% viewport height, scrollable content - **Dismissing**: Swipe down past threshold or tap backdrop - **Closed**: Slides down, backdrop fades ## Technical Implementation ### Using Framer Motion ```tsx function ToolBottomSheet({ tool, isOpen, onClose }) { return ( {isOpen && ( <> {/* Backdrop */} {/* Sheet */} { if (info.offset.y > 100 || info.velocity.y > 500) { onClose(); } }} className="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 z-50 max-h-[85vh] rounded-t-3xl bg-slate-900 overflow-hidden" > {/* Drag handle */}
{/* Content */}
{/* Tool details here */}
)} ); } ``` ### Responsive Integration ```tsx // In TldrToolCard const isMobile = useMediaQuery('(max-width: 767px)'); const [sheetOpen, setSheetOpen] = useState(false); // Different behavior based on viewport const handleExpand = () => { if (isMobile) { setSheetOpen(true); } else { onToggleExpand?.(); // Original inline expansion } }; ``` ## Files to Create/Modify - `components/tool-bottom-sheet.tsx` (new) - Bottom sheet component - `components/tldr-tool-card.tsx` - Conditional expansion behavior - `hooks/use-media-query.ts` (if not exists) - Media query hook ## Accessibility - Focus trap when sheet is open - Escape key closes sheet - `aria-modal="true"` on sheet - Return focus to trigger button on close - Backdrop click closes (with visual indication) ## Performance - Lazy load sheet content (only render when opening) - Use `will-change: transform` for smooth animation - Portal to document.body to escape stacking contexts ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Shows bottom sheet on mobile when expanding tool - [ ] Desktop retains inline expansion behavior - [ ] Sheet slides up with spring animation - [ ] Swipe down to dismiss works - [ ] Backdrop tap closes sheet - [ ] Focus trapped inside sheet - [ ] Escape key closes - [ ] Content scrollable within sheet - [ ] GitHub link accessible in sheet","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:52:25.875281504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:22:26.460230109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:22:26.460151013Z","close_reason":"Integrated mobile bottom sheet into TldrToolGrid. On mobile ( = {}; for (const { id, repo } of TOOL_REPOS) { const [owner, name] = repo.split('/'); const { data } = await octokit.repos.get({ owner, repo: name }); stars[id] = data.stargazers_count; console.log(`[STARS] ${id}: ${data.stargazers_count}`); } return stars; } // Write to lib/data/tldr-stars.json ``` ### Update tldr-data.ts ```typescript import starsData from './data/tldr-stars.json'; export const tldrTools: TldrTool[] = [ { id: 'ntm', stars: starsData.ntm ?? 0, // ... } ]; ``` ### Build script integration ```json { "scripts": { "prebuild": "bun run scripts/fetch-tool-stars.ts", "build": "next build" } } ``` ## Fallback Handling ```typescript // If stars data is missing or stale, use hardcoded fallbacks const DEFAULT_STARS: Record = { 'ntm': 69, 'agent-mail': 1400, // ... from content.ts }; export const getToolStars = (toolId: string): number => { return starsData[toolId] ?? DEFAULT_STARS[toolId] ?? 0; }; ``` ## Caching Strategy - Stars cached in JSON file - Updated on each build - Fallback to hardcoded values if fetch fails - No runtime API calls ## Error Handling ```typescript async function fetchStarsSafely() { try { return await fetchAllStars(); } catch (error) { console.warn('[STARS] Failed to fetch, using cached values'); return existingStarsData; } } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 13 tools have star counts - [ ] Stars update on each deploy - [ ] Build doesn't fail if GitHub API unavailable - [ ] Fallback values reasonable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:28:10.687009880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:17:44.646674015Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T18:17:44.646576424Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5s9","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-djw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5si","title":"Add animated data flow between flywheel nodes","description":"# Task: Animated Data Flow ## Objective Add visible animation showing how data/messages flow between tools in the flywheel. ## Current Behavior - Static lines connect tools - Hover shows connection exists ## Enhanced Behavior - Lines have subtle, continuous animation suggesting activity - On hover, the lines TO that tool pulse more prominently - Direction of flow is visible (arrows or particle direction) ## Animation Options ### Option A: Pulsing Lines - Lines have a gradient that slowly moves along the path - Simple, low-performance-cost - Shows directionality through gradient direction ### Option B: Particle Flow - Small dots travel along connection lines - More visually striking but higher perf cost - Clear directionality ### Option C: Glow Pulse - Connections glow briefly in sequence - Shows the "ripple" of data through the system - Medium complexity ## Recommended: Option A (Pulsing Gradient) - Best balance of visual impact and performance - Works well with prefers-reduced-motion (can be static) - Enhances existing design without overwhelming ## Implementation Notes - Use CSS animations or Framer Motion - Consider SVG stroke-dashoffset animation for gradient effect - Test on mobile for performance ## Files Affected - components/flywheel-visualization.tsx ## Dependencies - None (can work on existing component)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:04:47.673270271Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:23:01.668327319Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:23:01.668327319Z","close_reason":"Implemented continuous pulsing dash animation on flywheel connection lines using stroke-dasharray/dashoffset. Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion and speeds up when highlighted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} -{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5sjq","title":"Wills article smoke test should fail if a visualization stays on the loading skeleton","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:49.016168703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:49.016168703Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5sjq","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:49.016168703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5sjq","title":"Wills article smoke test should fail if a visualization stays on the loading skeleton","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:49.016168703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:19:07.001081301Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:19:06.990069931Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5sjq","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.30","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:49.016168703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w4","title":"Design GitHub heartbeat visual component","description":"## Overview Create the visual 'heartbeat' component that shows live GitHub activity status. ## Visual Concept Options 1. **Pulsing Dot**: Green dot that pulses when recently active 2. **Mini Contribution Graph**: Compact heatmap of last 7 days 3. **Activity Ticker**: Scrolling recent commits 4. **Status Badge**: 'Last commit: 2 hours ago' Recommend: Combination - Pulsing status dot + compact stats. ## Component Design ```typescript interface GitHubHeartbeatProps { activity: GitHubActivity; variant: 'minimal' | 'detailed' | 'full'; showLastCommit?: boolean; linkToProfile?: boolean; } ``` ## Visual States 1. **Active** (commit in last 24h): Green pulse 2. **Recent** (commit in last 7 days): Amber glow 3. **Idle** (no recent activity): Gray 4. **Loading**: Skeleton/shimmer 5. **Error**: Graceful fallback to static ## Animation - Subtle pulse animation for active state - Smooth transitions between states - Respects prefers-reduced-motion ## Integration - Can be placed in hero, stats grid, or footer - Should be small and non-intrusive - Links to GitHub profile on click ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All visual states implemented - [ ] Animations are smooth and subtle - [ ] Accessible (status conveyed to screen readers) - [ ] Works at different sizes/variants","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:09:46.220855480Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.710050300Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T08:05:51.710050300Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit b1cb57c: Two Worlds section with parallax effects, skill transfer cards; GitHub Heartbeat widget with live activity feed, animated heartbeat visualization, streak badges","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","title":"FEATURE: Design & Build UI Components for /tldr Page","description":"# FEATURE: Design & Build UI Components for /tldr Page ## Purpose Create the reusable React components needed for the /tldr page. These components must meet the highest UI/UX standards - this is a showcase page that demonstrates technical excellence. ## Components Required ### 1. TldrToolCard Component The main card component for displaying each tool. Must include: - Tool icon (from Lucide or custom) - Tool name and short tagline - Expandable/collapsible sections for: - "What it does" (always visible or prominent) - "Why it's useful" - "Implementation" (language, key tech) - "Synergies" (connected tools) - Visual indicator of "primary" vs "supporting" tool status - GitHub stars badge - Link to repo - Smooth hover states and transitions ### 2. TldrToolGrid Component Layout component that: - Arranges tool cards in responsive grid - Groups by category (Primary Flywheel / Supporting Tools) - Handles staggered entrance animations - Maintains visual hierarchy ### 3. TldrSynergyDiagram Component Interactive visualization showing: - All tools as nodes - Connections between synergistic tools - Hover to highlight connections - Click to filter/focus - Animated flow lines showing data/communication paths - Should be visually striking but not overwhelming ### 4. TldrHero Component Page header with: - Compelling headline about the flywheel concept - Brief intro paragraph - Visual element (maybe animated flywheel graphic) - Smooth scroll to tools section ## Design Principles - Consistent with existing site design language - Dark theme with slate colors, gradients - Accent colors per tool category (emerald for primary, violet for supporting, etc.) - Generous whitespace - Typography hierarchy - Microinteractions on every interactive element ## Animation Requirements - Entrance animations (staggered, spring physics) - Hover states (scale, glow, shadow changes) - Section expand/collapse (smooth height transitions) - Synergy diagram (flowing particles on connections) - Respect prefers-reduced-motion ## Accessibility - Keyboard navigable - ARIA labels on interactive elements - Focus indicators - Screen reader friendly ## Technical Notes - Use Framer Motion for animations - Follow existing component patterns (see endorsement-card.tsx, project-card.tsx) - Memoize where appropriate - Lazy load heavy components","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:13:32.193901182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T19:46:13.036333859Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T19:46:13.036333859Z","close_reason":"Completed - TLDR page fully implemented with all components and navigation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-1lj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.680974651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-8uk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.733973428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-gy1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.630736904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-uv7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.608590650Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-uw1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.655937231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-5w8","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-wsp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:30:06.706844238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]} unstable sort in RSS feed by handling NaN dates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} @@ -170,7 +170,7 @@ {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hco","title":"Refine Analytics hook condition","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-17T03:18:13.393577907Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T03:19:24.493246069Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T03:19:24.493246069Z","close_reason":"Added production check inside analytics useEffect.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","title":"Wills & Estate Planning skill launch article","description":"# Wills & Estate Planning skill — launch article + gated install UX ## What this epic covers Ship a full-length launch article at `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` that does two things at once: 1. **Promotes** the new `wills-and-estate-planning-skill` on jeffreys-skills.md (the biggest skill yet — 201 files, 572-line SKILL.md, 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, 135 reference docs, 8 operating modes, 5 wealth tiers, 11 axioms, 12 cognitive operators, 9 intake phases). Target audience is explicitly **non-technical** — office workers, parents, entrepreneurs, retirees — not just developers. 2. **Explains** the simplest-possible non-terminal install flow: paste a signed agent prompt into Claude Desktop or Codex Desktop, and the agent does the rest. Leverages the paid-jsm.md session in the users browser to gate bundle downloads via short-lived single-use codes. Mirrors the existing `jsm login` auth pattern. ## Why this matters The tax-preparation skill generated real user demand and real savings ($1k+ per user, with one user saving $20k). An X post teasing the wills skill got strong engagement. But these skills can only become mainstream if *non-developers* can install them. Todays jsm.md install UX requires `jsm install ` in a terminal, which excludes 90% of the target market. The article serves a secondary purpose: its a **case study in agent-friendly UX**. The paste-a-prompt-into-an-agent pattern generalizes to any paid skill on jsm.md, not just wills, so the article doubles as a template for future non-technical launches. ## Architectural pillars - **No public GitHub repo** for skill content. Everything gated behind jsm.md auth. - **Short-lived single-use install codes** minted server-side, bound to `(user_id, skill_id, exp, consumed_at)`. - **Agent-driven install**: the gated bundle URL is embedded in a copy-paste prompt that the users Claude/Codex agent executes. One tool-use approval on the desktop, no terminal keystrokes from the user. - **Works on every OS** (macOS / Windows / Linux) because the agent handles path/unzip — no native helper, no code signing required. - **Bundle watermark** inside the ZIPs README with user_id + timestamp. Deterrent against casual leaks; does not prevent determined piracy (acknowledged trade-off). ## Cross-repo scope - **This repo (`jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site`)**: the article + hero illustration + 5 custom visualizations + the agent-readable primer markdown in `/public/` + OG images + writing-index registration + command-palette entry + Playwright test. - **`/dp/jeffreys-skills.md`** (separate bead graph): DB migration + two endpoints + install-card UI + generic non-technical-user guide page. Tracked in that repos .beads/. ## Acceptance criteria - Article live at `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` with hero, TOC, 8+ long-form sections, 5 interactive visualizations, OG + Twitter images, and the tri-tab install section. - Primer markdown downloadable from the article. - Article visible in the writing index + command palette. - `bun run build` green, `bun tsc --noEmit` green, `bun lint` green, `ubs` green on changed files. - Playwright e2e smoke test covers: article renders, TOC links jump, visualizations mount, install-tab switches. - Depends on jsm.md beads being shipped for the install prompt URLs to resolve; acceptable to ship article first with a "coming soon" placeholder in the install section if jsm.md side is not ready.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:33:55.236585249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:33:55.236585249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","title":"Scaffold article route + metadata (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/page.tsx)","description":"Create `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/page.tsx` modeled on `app/writing/slack-mattermost-migration/page.tsx`: - `export const metadata` with title "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday | Jeffrey Emanuel" (or final chosen title), description, and `alternates.canonical` = `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`. - `articleSchema` JSON-LD (@type Article) with headline, datePublished (2026-04-XX on launch), author, publisher, mainEntityOfPage, `about` array including: Estate Planning, Wills, Trusts, AI Agents, Agent Skills, Claude Code, Codex, Probate, Incapacity Planning. - Default export renders `` and ``. ## Why this bead exists Next.js App Router needs the route stub in place before the component file can be referenced. Looks substantially like the slack articles equivalent block. - All skill-page URLs point at jeffreys-skills.md. - Heads-up paragraph about 404-unless-authenticated is present — this prevents confused readers from thinking the site is broken.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:03.276128406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:19:03.874763003Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:19:03.817637697Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.10","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:03.276128406Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.10","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.596037324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","title":"Install section with three tabs (agent paste / jsm CLI / manual ZIP)","description":"The single most important section of the article: the non-technical install flow. This is the payoff of the entire auth-code design. ## Section structure (id="install") SectionHeader eyebrow="Install" title="Get the skill into your agent in 90 seconds." Then three labeled tabs/cards (Id lean toward 3 stacked cards on mobile and a tab group on desktop — build it as 3 cards with subtle emphasis on card #1): ### Card 1: "In Claude or Codex Desktop (recommended, no terminal)" — default/emphasized 1. **Short pitch**: If youre not a developer, this is the only install path you need. You already have Claude Desktop or Codex Desktop installed (if not, link to the download pages). You have a paid jeffreys-skills.md subscription. Thats it. 2. **Step 1**: Visit the skill page on jeffreys-skills.md (link). Make sure youre signed in. 3. **Step 2**: Click `Generate install prompt`. The site mints a one-time, 10-minute-TTL, single-use code tied to your account and gives you a small block of text to copy. 4. **Step 3**: Open Claude or Codex Desktop. Paste the text. The agent reads it, asks you once to approve the single download tool call (standard "allow this command?" dialog), downloads the bundle, extracts it to the right folder, and tells you to reload skills. 5. **Step 4**: The skill appears in the skill picker. You type `/wills` or just describe what you want (`"I want to review my existing will — I moved from CA to TX last year"`) and the agent routes through the skill. **Callout**: `Why this works without a terminal` — "The agent IS the terminal. You never type a command; you approve one. The URL embedded in the prompt is a single-use token bound to your paid session, so even if you paste the prompt into someone elses computer it wont work there: the code expires before it can be reused." ### Card 2: "Via the jsm CLI (for developers)" - Same as slack articles pattern: install jsm, login, `jsm install wills-and-estate-planning-skill`. - Also mention `claude plugins install` alternative if/when that channel is supported. - Keep tight — this is the familiar path. ### Card 3: "Manual download (fallback)" - When the agent-paste flow doesnt work (e.g. the user is running Claude in Chrome or a constrained environment), they can click `Download bundle (.zip)` on the skill page. Same single-use code under the hood. - Instructions: unzip, then drag `wills-and-estate-planning-skill/` into `~/.claude/skills/` (macOS/Linux) or `%USERPROFILE%\.claude\skills\` (Windows). Same for `~/.codex/skills/` or `~/.agents/skills/` if using Codex. - Brief mention that theres a "reveal folder" helper button on the skill page that opens Finder/Explorer pointing at the right location. ## Pedagogical aside After card 3, a short explanatory paragraph about the auth-code flow: the signed URL is how jeffreys-skills.md proves to itself that the paid subscriber clicked the button. Its the same pattern as `jsm login` that developers already know, just re-aimed at install. ## Dependency / coordination This sections copy should mention specific URLs like `jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill`. The jsm.md-side beads need to have the install-card UI live before this sections instructions are technically accurate. Acceptable to ship the article with the UI partially live and update in a follow-up PR. ## Acceptance criteria - Three cards render correctly on mobile and desktop. - Each card has a clear CTA pointing at jsm.md. - No mock code blocks that imply the user must type bash — the agent does everything.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:07.466443691Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:30.447308428Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:51:16.260450848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.714423898Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.11","author":"ubuntu","text":"REVIEW 2026-04-18: Two strengthenings needed. (1) The article's install-section copy must match the actual text that appears on the jsm.md install card exactly (button label, TTL wording, generated-code format). If the article says 'click Generate install prompt' and the UI button says 'Get install prompt', readers will think the site is broken. Cross-check against bd-3t7zd.8 at implementation time. (2) Include explicit copy about failure modes: 'If the code expires before you paste it, go back and click Generate again.' / 'If the agent refuses the download step, click Approve.' / 'If the skill does not appear in the picker after reload, quit and reopen the app once.' Non-technical readers will hit every one of these at least some of the time; leaving them to figure it out by themselves erodes trust in the product.","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:57:07Z"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","title":"'Who this is for' section with tier-triage visualization","description":"Address the question every reader has after the install section: "does this apply to me?" ## Section structure (id="who") SectionHeader eyebrow="Scope" title="Who this is for (and who it isnt)." ### Opening paragraph Most people think estate planning is for rich people. It is not. Its for anyone who owns anything, cares about anyone, or would prefer a county court not decide what happens to their kids. The skill scales from Tier 1 (renter, mid-20s, no dependents) through Tier 5 ($100M+ industrialist with a private foundation), and the same interview adapts itself to whichever tier matches your facts. ### Embed the TierTriageViz visualization (Visualization implemented in a separate bead — `wills-estate-visualizations.tsx`.) Interactive: five rungs of a wealth ladder, each with: - Net worth band - Archetype ("young-family renter", "suburban homeowner + 401(k)", "founder with RSUs + SLAT candidate", "industrialist / dynasty") - Primary goals (guardianship, probate avoidance, transfer tax minimization, family governance) - Core deliverables (will, POAs, beneficiary audit → revocable trust + QTIP → GRAT + IDGT + dynasty trust, etc.) Tapping a rung expands an inline detail panel. Default selection should be Tier 2 (middle-class homeowner), since thats the modal reader. ### Complexity overlays Below the viz, a short list of "what bumps you up a tier regardless of net worth": - Blended family (+1) - Non-citizen spouse (+1, QDOT territory) - Privately held business (+1) - Disabled or vulnerable heir requiring SNT (+1) - Multi-state or multi-country assets (+1) - Specialty items: firearms (NFA trust), crypto self-custody, creator-economy IP, pre-IPO stock, addiction/capacity concerns. ### Who this is NOT for (honest scope limits) - Non-U.S. domiciliaries. The skill is U.S. federal + state + local; foreign-asset overlays exist but the core is U.S. law. - People who need a will *today* (bedside signing). The skill has an `urgent-bedside-signing` mode, but for true emergencies you want a lawyer on the phone, not an agent. - People who want a single-page "just write my will" interaction. The skill is deliberately thorough — it produces 45 artifacts — because coherence across documents is the whole point. If you just want a $30 LegalZoom will, go buy one. ## Acceptance criteria - Visualization renders and is keyboard-accessible. - Scope limits are explicit — reduces complaints post-launch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:52.573101216Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:24:29.193853884Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:24:29.190773721Z","close_reason":"Who-this-is-for section fully implemented in wills-estate-article.tsx lines 886-1007 with TierTriageViz wired in at line 905","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:37:52.573101216Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.384369815Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.12","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.838960023Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","title":"Core insight section: Axiom 0 + Axiom 1, one coherent story","description":"The thesis section. This is the part that, if the reader leaves with nothing else, should still change how they think about estate planning. ## Section structure (id="insight") SectionHeader eyebrow="The thesis" title="Why a will alone is not the plan." ### Narrative beats 1. **The most common failure mode is not a drafting error; it is incoherence across documents.** A beautifully-drafted will that says "everything to my kids" does nothing if the 401(k) still names an ex-spouse, because retirement accounts pass by *contract*, not by will. Axiom 0: the will does not control the whole estate. 2. **The plan is the whole story.** Will + revocable trust + beneficiary forms + deeds + POAs + healthcare directives + letter of instruction + digital inventory must all point at the same people, in the same shares, under the same contingencies, under the same states law. Axiom 1: one coherent story. 3. **Concrete example (drive it home)**: "Imagine you die tonight. Your spouse has to locate and call eight institutions in seven days: your 401(k) administrator, two IRA custodians, your life-insurance company, your countys register of deeds, your bank, your brokerage, and the guardian you named for your kids. Each of them will ask a different question. Each of them will accept a different document as proof. The skills job is to make sure the answer they get, and the document you produced, match — so your spouse doesnt spend six months learning probate law at the worst possible time." ### Embed the AxiomCoherenceViz visualization An interactive SVG/D3 diagram showing: - Center node: "You" - 8 spokes out to asset/decision categories (house, 401k, IRA, life insurance, brokerage, POAs, healthcare, digital). - Each spoke shows which document controls that asset (will / trust / beneficiary form / titling / POA / etc.). - Toggle "incoherence mode" that highlights mismatches — e.g., a will saying "to kids" but the 401(k) still naming the ex-spouse turns that spoke red. - Interactive: click a spoke, read a sentence-long plain-English explanation. ### Reinforce with 3 more axioms - **Axiom 2**: Plan for incapacity first, death second. Dementia, stroke, and coma damage more wealth than death, and lasts longer. - **Axiom 5**: Under the $15M exemption (2026 OBBBA), basis often dominates tax. Most families are better off *not* gifting appreciated assets out of the estate. - **Axiom 10**: Communication is the actual work. Surprise at the reading of the will is one of the fastest ways to turn a plan into litigation. (The other 7 axioms live in the primer and SKILL.md — dont try to cover all 11 here; the article is not a replacement for the skill.) ## Tone discipline - Every technical term should have a `` tooltip. - Use names and stakes. Not "elective share." "The rule that says your spouse can override your will and take roughly a third no matter what your will says." - No code. ## Acceptance criteria - Visualization mounts, works on touch devices, respects reduced-motion. - At least one reader test (a non-technical friend/family member) should read this section and be able to articulate "the will is not the plan" back to you.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:16.997426357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T14:39:01.128246134Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T14:39:01.125432706Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:38:16.997426357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.22","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:14.523855615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.13","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:44:12.961771157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}]} @@ -261,6 +261,7 @@ {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r37","title":"Pre-flight: Check for existing content to reuse for renamed projects","description":"## What Before adding ntm and slb entries, search the entire codebase for any existing descriptions, documentation, or content we can reuse from their original names (named_tmux_manager, simultaneous_launch_button). ## Why - User explicitly said: "ntm is a renaming of named_tmux_manager so keep that in mind and reuse the sections we already have written" - User explicitly said: "slb is a renaming of simultaneous_launch_button so keep that in mind and reuse the sections we already have written" - We must NOT lose any existing work or descriptions - Reusing existing content ensures consistency and saves effort ## Search Locations 1. `lib/content.ts` - Check projects array for named_tmux_manager or simultaneous_launch_button entries 2. `content/writing/` - Check markdown files for mentions or dedicated articles 3. Any other .md, .tsx, .ts files that might reference these projects 4. Check git history if entries were previously removed ## Implementation ```bash # Search for existing content grep -rn "named_tmux_manager\|simultaneous_launch_button" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.md" . grep -rn "tmux.*manager\|launch.*button" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" --include="*.md" . # Check git history for removed content git log --all --full-history -p -- '**/content.ts' | grep -A 20 -B 5 "named_tmux\|simultaneous_launch" ``` ## Output Create a document (or note in this bead) with: - Any existing descriptions found - Any existing tags found - Any blog posts or articles referencing these projects - Recommendation on what to reuse vs write fresh ## Dependencies - This task should be completed BEFORE adding ntm (ka2) and slb (ecn) ## Acceptance Criteria - Comprehensive search performed - Any existing content documented for reuse - Clear guidance provided for ntm and slb addition tasks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:33:54.770030767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T16:23:07.956002088Z","closed_at":"2026-01-12T16:23:07.956002088Z","close_reason":"Completed: Found and updated existing entries for ntm (Named Tmux Manager) and slb (Simultaneous Launch Button) - updated star counts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r63","title":"Add coding_agent_account_manager to content.ts (19 stars)","description":"## What Add coding_agent_account_manager (caam) project to content.ts ## Project Details - These tests ensure our project additions don't introduce errors and maintain quality standards. ## Why - Catch errors early before they reach production - Ensure all required fields are present - Prevent duplicate entries - Validate URLs are properly formatted - Ensure consistent tag usage across projects - Verify badge format consistency - Document expected data structure through tests ## Test File Location Create: `jeffreyemanuel-site/__tests__/content.test.ts` (or `tests/content.test.ts`) ## Test Categories with Detailed Specifications ### 1. Schema Validation Tests ```typescript describe('Project Schema Validation', () => { test('all projects have required fields', () => { // Required: name, kind, href, short, description, tags projects.forEach(project => { expect(project.name).toBeDefined(); expect(project.name.length).toBeGreaterThan(0); expect(['product', 'oss', 'research']).toContain(project.kind); expect(project.href).toBeDefined(); expect(project.short).toBeDefined(); expect(project.description).toBeDefined(); expect(Array.isArray(project.tags)).toBe(true); expect(project.tags.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2); }); }); test('optional fields have correct types when present', () => { // badge: string, size: enum, gradient: string }); }); ``` ### 2. URL Validation Tests ```typescript describe('URL Validation', () => { test('all project hrefs are valid URLs', () => { projects.forEach(project => { expect(() => new URL(project.href)).not.toThrow(); expect(project.href).toMatch(/^https?:\/\//); }); }); test('no duplicate hrefs exist', () => { const hrefs = projects.map(p => p.href); const uniqueHrefs = new Set(hrefs); expect(uniqueHrefs.size).toBe(hrefs.length); }); test('GitHub URLs use correct format', () => { // https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ }); }); ``` ### 3. Content Quality Tests ```typescript describe('Content Quality', () => { test('short descriptions are actually short (< 150 chars)', () => { projects.forEach(project => { expect(project.short.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(150); expect(project.short.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(20); }); }); test('descriptions are substantive (50-500 chars)', () => { projects.forEach(project => { expect(project.description.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(50); expect(project.description.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(500); }); }); test('no duplicate project names', () => { const names = projects.map(p => p.name.toLowerCase()); const uniqueNames = new Set(names); expect(uniqueNames.size).toBe(names.length); }); }); ``` ### 4. Badge Consistency Tests ```typescript describe('Badge Consistency', () => { test('star badges follow consistent format', () => { // Format: "123 stars" or "1.2K stars" or "2.8K stars" const starBadgePattern = /^(\d+\.?\d*K?)\s+stars$/; projects.forEach(project => { if (project.badge && project.badge.includes('star')) { expect(project.badge).toMatch(starBadgePattern); } }); }); }); ``` ### 5. Tag Consistency Tests ```typescript describe('Tag Consistency', () => { test('tags use consistent capitalization', () => { // All tags should be Title Case or consistent format }); test('no overly long tags (max 20 chars)', () => { projects.forEach(project => { project.tags.forEach(tag => { expect(tag.length).toBeLessThanOrEqual(20); }); }); }); test('common tags are spelled consistently', () => { // Check for variations like "AI" vs "ai" vs "Ai" // "DevTools" vs "Dev Tools" vs "devtools" }); }); ``` ### 6. heroStats Validation Tests ```typescript describe('heroStats Validation', () => { test('project count matches actual projects array', () => { const statsValue = heroStats.find(s => s.label.includes('project'))?.value; const count = parseInt(statsValue?.replace(/[^0-9]/g, '') || '0'); // Should be within reasonable range of actual count expect(count).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(projects.length - 10); expect(count).toBeLessThanOrEqual(projects.length + 10); }); }); ``` ## Logging Requirements - Each test should log what it's checking: `console.log(\`Checking project: ${project.name}\`)` - Failed assertions should log detailed context - Summary at end: "Validated X projects, Y tags, Z URLs" ## Test Runner Configuration ```json // package.json addition { "scripts": { "test:content": "bun test __tests__/content.test.ts --verbose" } } ``` ## Dependencies - Should be created AFTER all project additions - Must complete BEFORE final verification ## Acceptance Criteria - Test file created with all test categories above - Tests are comprehensive (cover all edge cases) - Tests pass with current valid content - Tests have clear, descriptive names - Logging provides visibility into test execution - Can be run via `bun test:content`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T15:34:27.816708505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T02:02:12.311640814Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T02:02:12.311640814Z","close_reason":"Created comprehensive content validation tests in tests/unit/content-validation.test.ts: -
